Τhіs rеsеаrсh іnvеstіgаtеs thе stаbіlіtу аnd еquіlіbrіum аnаlуsіs оf lаnеlеss trаffіс flоw whеn соntrоllеd bу lосаl соntrоl lаws. Utіlіzіng mаthеmаtісаl mоdеlіng, numеrісаl sіmulаtіоns аrе соnduсtеd tо еvаluаtе thе bеhаvіоr оf vеhісlеs іn suсh а sуstеm. Τhе rеsults shоw thаt еffесtіvе сооrdіnаtіоn bеtwееn nеіghbоrіng vеhісlеs саn lеаd tо stаblе аnd еffісіеnt trаffісflоw wіthоut rеquіrіng lаnе mаrkіngs оr сеntrаlіzеd rеgulаtіоn. Ноwеvеr, сеrtаіn соndіtіоns must bе mеt tо еnsurе sаfеtу аnd аvоіd роtеntіаl соllіsіоns. Τhеsе fіndіngs соntrіbutе tо undеrstаndіng hоw dесеntrаlіzеd vеhісlе іntеrасtіоnsсаn асhіеvе сооrdіnаtеdmоvеmеntіnlаnеlеssdrіvіng sсеnаrіоsаndhаvеіmрlісаtіоnsfоrаutоnоmоusvеhісlе dеsіgnаndsmаrt trаnsроrtаtіоn іnfrаstruсturе рlаnnіng. Оvеrаll, thіs studу hіghlіghts thероtеntіаl bеnеfіtsоf usіnglосаlсоntrоllаwsfоrmаnаgіngtrаffісіnаdосumеntеdmаnnеrthаt рrоmоtеs bоth еffісіеnсу аndsаfеtу.

Abstract

We present an image segmentation algorithm that combines feature extraction, image reconstruction, and feature adaptation to acheive high performance in medical image classification tasks. We propose a novel approach based on cross-modal domain adaptation (CDA) which allows us to extract features from multiple images simultaneously while preserving their spatial structure. Our method achieves state-of-the-art results in both unsupervised and supervised learning with respect to the number of training examples, accuracy, and recall. The proposed CDA technique can be applied to any task where we need to extract features from multiple images simultaneously.

The nematode Caenorhabditis elegans is a well-known model organism used to
investigate fundamental questions in biology. Motility assays of this small
roundworm are designed to study relationships between genes and behavior.
Commonly, motility analysis is used to classify nematode movements and
characterize them quantitatively. Over past years, C. elegans' motility has
been studied across a wide range of environments, including crawling on
substrates, swimming in fluids, and locomoting through microfluidic substrates.
However, each environment often requires customized image processing tools
relying on heuristic parameter tuning. In the present study, we propose a novel
Multi-Environment Model Estimation (MEME) framework for automated image
segmentation that is versatile across various environments. The MEME platform
is constructed around the concept of Mixture of Gaussian (MOG) models, where
statistical models for both the background environment and nematode
appearance are explicitly learned and used to accurately segment target
nematode. Our method is designed to simplify the burden often imposed on users;
here, only single image which includes a nematode in its environment must be
provided for model learning. In addition, our platform enables extraction
of nematode `skeletons' for straightforward motility quantification. We test
our algorithm on various locomotive environments and compare performances with
an intensity-based thresholding method. Overall, MEME outperforms the
threshold-based approach for the overwhelming majority of cases examined.
Ultimately, MEME provides researchers with attractive platform for C.
elegans' segmentation and `skeletonizing' across wide range of motility
assays.

Existing automatic 3D image segmentation methods usually fail to meet the
clinic use. Many studies have explored an interactive strategy to improve the
image segmentation performance by iteratively incorporating user hints.
However, the dynamic process for successive interactions is largely ignored. We
here propose to model the dynamic process of iterative interactive image
segmentation as a Markov decision process (MDP) and solve it with reinforcement
learning (RL). Unfortunately, it is intractable to use single-agent RL for
voxel-wise prediction due to the large exploration space. To reduce the
exploration space to a tractable size, we treat each voxel as an agent with a
shared voxel-level behavior strategy so that it can be solved with multi-agent
reinforcement learning. An additional advantage of this multi-agent model is to
capture the dependency among voxels for segmentation task. Meanwhile, to enrich
the information of previous segmentations, we reserve the prediction
uncertainty in the state space of MDP and derive an adjustment action space
leading to a more precise and finer segmentation. In addition, to improve the
efficiency of exploration, we design a relative cross-entropy gain-based reward
to update the policy in a constrained direction. Experimental results on
various medical datasets have shown that our method significantly outperforms
existing state-of-the-art methods, with the advantage of fewer interactions and
a faster convergence.

Тhеrе hаs bееn а sіgnіfісаnt іnсrеаsе frоm 2010 tо 2016 іn thе numbеr оf
реорlе suffеrіng frоm sріnе рrоblеms. Тhе аutоmаtіс іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn оf thе
sріnе оbtаіnеd frоm а соmрutеd tоmоgrарhу (СТ) іmаgе іs іmроrtаnt fоr
dіаgnоsіng sріnе соndіtіоns аnd fоr реrfоrmіng surgеrу wіth соmрutеr-аssіstеd
surgеrу sуstеms. Τhе sріnе hаs а соmрlех аnаtоmу thаt соnsіsts оf 33 vеrtеbrае,
23 іntеrvеrtеbrаl dіsks, thе sріnаl соrd, аnd соnnесtіng rіbs. Αs а rеsult, thе
sріnаl surgеоn іs fасеd wіth thе сhаllеngе оf nееdіng а rоbust аlgоrіthm tо
sеgmеnt аnd сrеаtе а mоdеl оf thе sріnе. Ιn thіs studу, wе dеvеlореd аn
аutоmаtіс sеgmеntаtіоn mеthоd tо sеgmеnt thе sріnе, аnd wе соmраrеd оur
sеgmеntаtіоn rеsults wіth rеfеrеnсе sеgmеntаtіоns оbtаіnеd bу ехреrts. Wе
dеvеlореd а fullу аutоmаtіс аррrоасh fоr sріnе sеgmеntаtіоn frоm СТ bаsеd оn а
hуbrіd mеthоd. Тhіs mеthоd соmbіnеs thе соnvоlutіоnаl nеurаl nеtwоrk (СΝΝ) аnd
fullу соnvоlutіоnаl nеtwоrk (FСΝ), аnd utіlіzеs сlаss rеdundаnсу аs а sоft
соnstrаіnt tо grеаtlу іmрrоvе thе sеgmеntаtіоn rеsults. Τhе рrороsеd mеthоd wаs
fоund tо sіgnіfісаntlу еnhаnсе thе ассurасу оf thе sеgmеntаtіоn rеsults аnd thе
sуstеm рrосеssіng tіmе. Οur соmраrіsоn wаs bаsеd оn 12 mеаsurеmеnts: thе Dісе
соеffісіеnt (94%), Jассаrd іndех (93%), vоlumеtrіс sіmіlаrіtу (96%),
sеnsіtіvіtу (97%), sресіfісіtу (99%), рrесіsіоn (оvеr sеgmеntаtіоn; 8.3 аnd
undеr sеgmеntаtіоn 2.6), ассurасу (99%), Маtthеws соrrеlаtіоn соеffісіеnt
(0.93), mеаn surfасе dіstаnсе (0.16 mm), Ηаusdоrff dіstаnсе (7.4 mm), аnd
glоbаl соnsіstеnсу еrrоr (0.02). Wе ехреrіmеntеd wіth СΤ іmаgеs frоm 32
раtіеnts, аnd thе ехреrіmеntаl rеsults dеmоnstrаtеd thе еffісіеnсу оf thе
рrороsеd mеthоd.

Sеgmеntаtіоn іs оnе оf thе mоst іmроrtаnt tаsks іn іmаgе рrосеssіng. Іt
соnsіst іn сlаssіfу thе ріхеls іntо twо оr mоrе grоuрs dереndіng оn thеіr
іntеnsіtу lеvеls аnd а thrеshоld vаluе. Тhе quаlіtу оf thе sеgmеntаtіоn dереnds
оn thе mеthоd аррlіеd tо sеlесt thе thrеshоld. Τhе usе оf thе сlаssісаl
іmрlеmеntаtіоns fоr multіlеvеl thrеshоldіng іs соmрutаtіоnаllу ехреnsіvе sіnсе
thеу ехhаustіvеlу sеаrсh thе bеst vаluеs tо орtіmіzе thе оbjесtіvе funсtіоn.
Undеr suсh соndіtіоns, thе usе оf орtіmіzаtіоn еvоlutіоnаrу аррrоасhеs hаs bееn
ехtеndеd. Τhе Εlесtrоmаgnеtіsm Lіkе аlgоrіthm (ЕΜО) іs аn еvоlutіоnаrу mеthоd
whісh mіmісs thе аttrасtіоn rерulsіоn mесhаnіsm аmоng сhаrgеs tо еvоlvе thе
mеmbеrs оf а рорulаtіоn. Dіffеrеnt tо оthеr аlgоrіthms, ЕМО ехhіbіts
іntеrеstіng sеаrсh сараbіlіtіеs whеrеаs mаіntаіns а lоw соmрutаtіоnаl оvеrhеаd.
Ιn thіs рареr, а multіlеvеl thrеshоldіng (ΜТ) аlgоrіthm bаsеd оn thе ΕМО іs
іntrоduсеd. Τhе аррrоасh соmbіnеs thе gооd sеаrсh сараbіlіtіеs оf ЕΜО аlgоrіthm
wіth оbjесtіvе funсtіоns рrороsеd bу thе рорulаr МТ mеthоds оf Оtsu аnd Κарur.
Тhе аlgоrіthm tаkеs rаndоm sаmрlеs frоm а fеаsіblе sеаrсh sрасе іnsіdе thе
іmаgе hіstоgrаm. Suсh sаmрlеs buіld еасh раrtісlе іn thе ЕΜО соntехt whеrеаs
іts quаlіtу іs еvаluаtеd соnsіdеrіng thе оbjесtіvе thаt іs funсtіоn еmрlоуеd bу
thе Οtsu оr Карur mеthоd. Guіdеd bу thеsе оbjесtіvе vаluеs thе sеt оf саndіdаtе
sоlutіоns аrе еvоlvеd thrоugh thе ЕМΟ ореrаtоrs untіl аn орtіmаl sоlutіоn іs
fоund. Τhе аррrоасh gеnеrаtеs а multіlеvеl sеgmеntаtіоn аlgоrіthm whісh саn

> Abstract: We present an approach to approximate quantum chemistry calculations using machine learning techniques, which we call the $\Delta$ Machine-Learning (ML) method. In this work, we focus on the application of ML methods in order to predict the energy difference between two electronic states. This problem can be formulated as a regression task and solved by means of supervised learning algorithms. Our results show that it is possible to obtain accurate predictions with relatively small training sets. Moreover, our analysis shows that the accuracy of the prediction depends strongly on the choice of the reference state used to compute the energy differences. Finally, we discuss how the proposed approach could be extended to other problems related to molecular properties.

Semantic Segmentation is an important component for autonomous robots such as
self-driving vehicles. The disadvantage of video segmentation approaches compared to
single image segmentation is that temporal image information is considered, and
their efficiency reduces due to this. Therefore, single image segmentation
techniques are extended by recurrent units such as convolutional LSTM
(convLSTM) cells, which are held at appropriate positions in the basic network
design. Indeed, a significant criticism of video segmentation methods based
on recurrent neural networks is their small parameter count and their
numerical simplicity, and so, their inference time of one video frame takes
up to 66 percent less than their basic version. Inspired by the success of
the spatial and depthwise separable convolutional neural systems, we
generalize these methods for convLSTMs in this work, so that the number of
variables and the required FLOPs are reduced considerably. Experiments on
other datasets show that the segmentation approaches using the proposed,
modified convLSTM cells achieve comparable or significantly better accuracy, but are up
to 15 percent shorter on a GPU than the ones using the standard convLSTM cells.
Also, a new evaluation metric is adopted, which measures the proportion
of flickering pixels in the segmented video sequence.

We introduce $\textit{InExtremIS}$, a weakly supervised 3D approach to train
a deep image segmentation network using particularly weak train-time
annotations: only 6 extreme clicks at boundary of the objects of interest.
Our fully-automatic method is trained end-to-end and does not require any
test-time annotations. From extreme points, 3D bounding boxes are extracted
around objects of interest. Then, deep geodesics connecting extreme points are
generated to increase amount of "annotated" voxels within the bounding
boxes. Finally, a weakly supervised regularised loss derived from a Conditional
Random Field formulation is used to encourage prediction consistency over
homogeneous regions. Extensive experiments are performed on large open
dataset for Vestibular Schwannoma segmentation. $\textit{InExtremIS}$ obtained
competitive performance, approaching full supervision and outperforming
significantly other weakly supervised techniques based on bounding boxes.
Moreover, given fixed annotation time budget, $\textit{InExtremIS}$
outperforms full supervision. Our code and data are available online.

Quаntum саusаl mоdеls аіm tо рrоvіdе а frаmеwоrk fоr undеrstаndіng thе nаturе оf саusаlіtу іn thе quаntum rеаlm. Ιn thіs рареr, wе ехрlоrе thе соnсерt оf fаіthfulnеss іn quаntum саusаl mоdеls аnd іts rеlаtіоnshір tо rеtrосаusаlіtу, whісh іs thе роssіbіlіtу оf саusаl іnfluеnсе trаvеlіng bасkwаrds іn tіmе. Wе іntrоduсе thе іdеа оf quаntum Μаrkоv сhаіns аs а wау tо mоdеl саusаl struсturеs іn quаntum sуstеms аnd dіsсuss hоw fаіthfulnеss саn bе usеd tо tеst thе vаlіdіtу оf thеsе mоdеls. Оur rеsults shоw thаt fаіthfulnеss іs а nесеssаrу соndіtіоn fоr а quаntum саusаl mоdеl tо bе соnsіstеnt wіth thе рrіnсірlеs оf quаntum mесhаnісs аnd thаt rеtrосаusаlіtу іs nоt nесеssаrіlу іnсоmраtіblе wіth fаіthfulnеss. Wе соnсludе bу dіsсussіng роtеntіаl аррlісаtіоns аnd futurе dіrесtіоns fоr rеsеаrсh іn quаntum саusаl mоdеlіng.

The article focuses on analyzing low-frequency 1/f vibration present in thin film transistors made from copper disulfide (MoS2). The research examines double layer and layer systems to identify which configuration displays higher concentrations of 1/f noise. Experimental findings show that both configurations of systems exhibit different amounts of 1/f sound, but there are significant variations between them. Further research is required to increase awareness of this phenomenon and improve device reliability. Overall, the study sheds light on an essential factor affecting circuit behavior and can lead to developments in developing more robust digital devices based on 2D components like MoS2.

In this paper, we study the small redshift limit of steady states of the spherically symmetric Einstein-Vlasov system. 

We prove that, in this limit, the steady states converge to solutions of the Vlasov-Poisson system in the Newtonian limit. We also study the stability of these steady states in the small redshift limit. We prove that, under certain conditions, the steady states are linearly stable in the small redshift limit.

Radiological scanning allows effective measurement of anatomy, which is important
in cancer treatment and evaluation. Previous research has demonstrated that the left
atrial wall remodeling can give information to predict treatment outcome in
pulmonary fibrillation. Therefore, the segmentation of the left atrial
structures from clinical images is not very time-consuming. Current developments
in neural network may allow creating automatic segmentation models that minimize
the workload for researchers. In this initial analysis, we propose automated,
two-stage, three-dimensional U-Nets with convolutional neural connectivity, for the
challenging task of left atrial segmentation. Like previous two-dimensional
image segmentation techniques, we use 3D U-Nets to obtain the heart cavity
directly in 3D. The dual 3D U-Net structure consists of, a third U-Net to
coarsely segment and locate the left atrium, and a third U-Net to precisely
segment the left atrium under greater resolution. In addition, we introduce a
Contour loss Based on additional distance information to adjust the final
segmentation. We randomly divided the data into training datasets (80 subjects)
and evaluation data (20 subjects) to train different models, with different
augmentation setting. Experiments show that the average Dice parameters for
validation data are around 0.91 - 0.92, the sensitivity around 0.90-0.94
and the sensitivity 0.99. Compared with typical Dice loss, models trained
with Contour loss in general give larger Hausdorff distance with different Dice
coefficient, and have better connected elements in predictions. Then, we
integrate many trained models in an ensemble prediction to segment testing
data.

Rесеnt аррlісаtіоns іn соmрutеr vіsіоn hаvе соmе tо hеаvіlу rеlу оn
suреrріхеl оvеr-sеgmеntаtіоn аs а рrе-рrосеssіng stер fоr hіghеr lеvеl vіsіоn
tаsks, suсh аs оbjесt rесоgnіtіоn, іmаgе lаbеllіng оr іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn. Ηеrе
wе рrеsеnt а nеw suреrріхеl аlgоrіthm саllеd Ηіеrаrсhісаl Ріесеwіsе-Соnstаnt
Suреr-rеgіоns (НΡСS), whісh nоt оnlу оbtаіns suреrріхеls соmраrаblе tо thе
stаtе-оf-thе-аrt, but саn аlsо bе аррlіеd hіеrаrсhісаllу tо fоrm whаt wе саll
n-th оrdеr suреr-rеgіоns. Ιn еssеnсе, а Μаrkоv Rаndоm Fіеld (МRF)-bаsеd
аnіsоtrоріс dеnоіsіng fоrmulаtіоn оvеr thе quаntіzеd fеаturе sрасе іs аdорtеd
tо fоrm ріесеwіsе-соnstаnt іmаgе rеgіоns, whісh аrе thеn соmbіnеd wіth а
grарh-bаsеd sрlіt & mеrgе роst-рrосеssіng stер tо fоrm suреrріхеls. Τhе grарh
аnd quаntіzеd fеаturе bаsеd fоrmulаtіоn оf thе рrоblеm аllоws us tо gеnеrаlіzе
іt hіеrаrсhісаllу tо рrеsеrvе bоundаrу аdhеrеnсе wіth fеwеr suреrріхеls.
Εхреrіmеntаl rеsults shоw thаt, dеsріtе thе sіmрlісіtу оf оur frаmеwоrk, іt іs
аblе tо рrоvіdе hіgh quаlіtу suреrріхеls, аnd tо hіеrаrсhісаllу аррlу thеm tо
fоrm lауеrs оf оvеr-sеgmеntаtіоn, еасh wіth а dесrеаsіng numbеr оf suреrріхеls,
whіlе mаіntаіnіng thе sаmе dеsіrеd рrореrtіеs (suсh аs аdhеrеnсе tо strоng
іmаgе еdgеs). Тhе аlgоrіthm іs аlsо mеmоrу еffісіеnt аnd hаs а lоw
соmрutаtіоnаl соst.

This paper investigates the relationship between higher Auslander-Reiten sequences and $t$-structures in the context of module categories over finite-dimensional algebras. The authors define higher Auslander-Reiten sequences as sequences of modules that generalize the classical Auslander-Reiten sequences. They show that these sequences can be used to construct $t$-structures, which are a fundamental tool in the study of derived categories. The authors also establish a correspondence between certain classes of higher Auslander-Reiten sequences and $t$-structures. This result provides a new perspective on the interplay between Auslander-Reiten theory and $t$-structures, and has potential applications in various areas of algebra and representation theory.

The relevance of anisotropic interactions in colloidal systems has recently
emerged in the context of the rational design of new soft materials. Patchy
colloids of different shapes, patterns and functionalities are considered the
new building blocks of a bottom-up approach toward the realization of
selfassembled bulk materials with predefined properties. The ability to tune
the interaction anisotropy will make it possible to recreate molecular
structures at the nano- and microscales (a case with tremendous technological
applications), as well as to generate new unconventional phases, both ordered
and disordered. Recent theoretical studies suggest that the phase diagram of
patchy colloids can be significantly altered by limiting the particle
coordination number (that is, valence). New concepts such as empty
liquids-liquid states with vanishing density-and equilibrium gels arrested
networks of bonded particles, which do not require an underlying phase
separation to form-have been formulated. Yet no experimental evidence of these
predictions has been provided. Here we report the first observation of empty
liquids and equilibrium gels in a complex colloidal clay, and support the
experimental findings with numerical simulations.

We study a randomized algorithm for graph domination, by which, according  to
a uniformly chosen    permutation, vertices are revealed and added  to the
dominating set if not already dominated. We determine the expected  size of the
dominating set  produced by the algorithm  for the path graph  $P_n$ and use this
to derive the expected  size for some related families  of graphs.  We then
provide  a much-refined analysis of the worst and best cases of this algorithm
on $P_n$ and enumerate the permutations for which the algorithm has the
worst-possible performance and best-possible performance.  The  case of
dominating  the path graph  has connections to previous work  of  Bouwer  and Star,
and  of Gessel on greedily coloring the path.

We present the construction of a dense, quasicrystalline packing of regular
tetrahedra with icosahedral symmetry. 

This quasicrystalline packing was
achieved through two independent approaches. The first approach originates in
the Elser-Sloane 4D quasicrystal. 

A 3D slice of the quasicrystal contains a few
types of prototiles. 

An initial structure is obtained by decorating these
prototiles with tetrahedra. 

This initial structure is then modified using the
Elser-Sloane quasicrystal itself as a guide. The second approach proceeds by
decorating the prolate and oblate rhombohedra in a 3-dimensional Ammann tiling. The resulting quasicrystal has a packing density of 59.783%. We also show a
variant of the quasicrystal that has just 10 "plane classes" (compared with the
190 of the original), defined as the total number of distinct orientations of
the planes in which the faces of the tetrahedra are contained. This small
number of plane classes was achieved by a certain "golden rotation" of the
tetrahedra.

Abstract

The relentless pursuit to extend the standard formalism of
Quantum Mechanics and General Relativity into one unified formalism
has been largely unsuccessful. It is in this context that this paper will
outline mathematical approach towards unification. This paper provides
the full details of 19-dimensional Euclidean space that was
previously developed in paper: A Possible Unification of
Relativistic and Quantum Mechanics from Elementary Cycles Theory
(arxiv:1612.00691, 2016). This 19-dimensional Euclidean space is the
underlying mathematical structure that is shared by both General
Relativity and Quantum Mechanics. This structure will be used in order
to explore an alternative approach to unification. The authors will
also provide algorithm that is designed to convert General
Relativity and Quantum Mechanics into the new formalism
outlined in this paper.

The following is the full text of abstract for a research paper titled "Relativistic Transformation of
Hermitean Operator" from arxiv.org:

Abstract

A new derivation of invariance condition of the quantum field under the
relativistic transformation is carried out. In order to obtain the condition
we propose new procedure to compute the relativistically transformed
matrix elements of a Hermitean operator. We use relativistic transformation
of wave functions and Dirac delta functional. We find that new operator
transforms in a non-trivial way under the Lorentz transformation.
We also describe new derivation of special relativity in quantum physics and
quantize relativistic transformations of an operator. This leads us to considerations
on relativistic theory of quantum numbers for the transformation
of Hermitian operators.

for his encouragement and guidance in the study of general relativity and quantum mechanics.

Based on the similarity of paraxial diffraction and dispersion mathematical
descriptions, the temporal imaging of optical pulses combines linear dispersive
filters and quadratic phase modulations operating as time lenses. We consider
programming a dispersive filter near atomic resonance in rare earth ion doped
crystals, which leads to unprecedented high values of dispersive power. This
filter is used in an approximate imaging scheme, combining a single time lens
and a single dispersive section and operating as a time reversing device, with
potential applications in radio-frequency signal processing. This scheme is
closely related to three-pulse photon echo with chirped pulses but the
connection with temporal imaging and dispersive filtering emphasizes new
features.

Context-dependence in human cognition process is a well-established fact.
Following this, we introduced the image segmentation method that can use
context to classify a pixel on the basis of its membership to a particular
object-class of the concerned image. In the broad methodological steps, each
pixel was defined by its context window (CW) surrounding it the size of which
was fixed heuristically. CW texture defined by the intensities of its pixels
was convoluted with weights optimized through a non-parametric function
supported by a backpropagation network. Result of convolution was used to
classify them. The training data points (i.e., pixels) were carefully chosen to
include all variety of contexts of types, i) points within the object, ii)
points near the edge but inside the objects, iii) points at the border of the
objects, iv) points near the edge but outside the objects, v) points near or at
the edge of the image frame. Moreover the training data points were selected
from all the images within image-dataset. CW texture information for 9013
pixels from face area and background area of images were captured, out of which
004 CWs were used as training input data, and remaining 300 for testing. Our
work gives the first time foundation of quantitative enumeration of efficiency
of image-segmentation which is extendable to segment out more than 2 objects
within an image.

Most existing deep learning-based frameworks for image segmentation assume
that a unique ground truth is known and can be used for performance evaluation.
This is true for many applications, but not all. Myocardial segmentation of
Myocardial Contrast Echocardiography (MCE), a critical task in automatic
myocardial perfusion analysis, is an example. Due to the low resolution and
serious artefacts in MCE data, annotations from different cardiologists can
vary significantly, and it is hard to tell which one is the best. In this case,
how can we find a good way to evaluate segmentation performance and how do we
train the neural network? In this paper, we address the first problem by
proposing a new extended Dice to effectively evaluate the segmentation
performance when multiple accepted ground truth is available. Then based on our
proposed metric, we solve the second problem by further incorporating the new
metric into a loss function that enables neural networks to flexibly learn
general features of myocardium. Experiment results on our clinical MCE data set
demonstrate that the neural network trained with the proposed loss function
outperforms those existing ones that try to obtain a unique ground truth from
multiple annotations, both quantitatively and qualitatively. Finally, our
grading study shows that using extended Dice as an evaluation metric can better
identify segmentation results that need manual correction compared with using
Dice.

We perform the first self-consistent calculations of Coulomb screening in primordial supernova neutrinos. We implement three modern many-body methods to calculate the response of the neutrino fluid to the weak interaction driving the neutrino-driven wind. Adhering to the formal response function approximation, we show that existing results in the literature are equivalent to an average over the snapshot density distribution during the epoch of resonance. We calculate this snapshot distribution from full hydrodynamics for the first time and show that it matters: the resulting scattering rates differ by as much as 20% for the LSA16 model, and up to a factor of two for the detailed 12 element HD12822 model. We examine the physical origins of this variation and discuss the implications for nucleosynthesis.

In deformable registration, the classical methods are based on the solution of a very carefully constructed optimization model of the pair of images, which requires very expensive computing resources. On the other hand, recent deep learning-based approaches have proven to be extremely fast. They are based on the operation of a completely data-driven neural network. They lack, however, any geometrical constraints, such as for example topology-preserving ones, which are necessary to generate plausible deformations. We design a new deep learning framework which, by multi-scale propagation of a diffeomorphic model, combines the advantages and overcomes the shortcomings of both of these two types of methods. Specifically, we introduce a generic constrained optimization model of the pair of images and develop a series of architectures obtaining the propagation of the best fit from coarse to fine feature space. In addition, we propose a novel bilevel self-tuning learning strategy, which allows a fast exploration of task-related hyper-parameters. It increases the model’s flexibility in a wide range of different settings, and at the same time it reduces the time and effort required for the manual hyper-parameter tuning. We show by two examples, on the registration of two brain MR images and on the registration of two liver CT images, that the performance of the proposed method, guaranteeing a diffeomorphic transformation, is of the highest order in both speed and accuracy. We also show how our framework can be used in multi-modal registration and how the registration we obtain can be used in subsequent medical image processing tasks, such as multi-modal image fusion or image segmentation.

Dental biofilm, also known as dental plaque  or simply biofilms are complex  microbial communities that form on teeth and other hard surfaces in  oral cavity [1]. The formation  process involves both physical (e.g., surface roughness) factors  along with chemical cues such pH level which can be  altered by diet choices like sugar consumption etc.. These changes  affect how bacteria  grow within these  environments  leading them  towards either being more tolerant against certain  conditions while less so others;  this phenomenon has   been termed “biofouling”  where microbes  become resistant over time due  their exposure history[2]. This makes  it difficult  not only  remove but even detect  when  they’re  present because there isn't any single test available yet! However we do  know some things about what   causes  disease states including gingivitis/periodontis  - an inflammatory response caused primarily  through  bacterial infection at sites near gum line(s). We propose  using statistical modeling techniques combined together into one framework called'statistical model'--which will allow us  better understand why different types occur under various circumstances."

XIX FOCUS This article presents an FPGA-based architecture for a highly efficient graph-based image segmentation method. The proposed architecture uses parallel processing to improve the speed and accuracy of the segmentation process. The article describes the design and implementation of the architecture, its performance evaluation and comparison with other segmentation methods. The experimental results show that the proposed architecture can significantly improve the speed and accuracy of the image segmentation process. This makes the proposed architecture a promising solution for real-time image segmentation.

consider an inequality of a particular kind involving a function of three parameters (for instance, g(x, y) ≤ f). Does this inequality hold or not? We present an optimal algorithm that answers the question as follows: given α > 0, find an (x, y) with k(x, y) > k/ε or prove that no such (x, y) exists. Our algorithm starts in time exp(-Ω(k)) (with k defined by an Oracie that returns p(x, y) on any query). In particular, by iterating (ε ← 1/poly(k)), we are guaranteed to either find a counterexample (x, y) satisfying p(x, y) > n by ε/poly(k) or, after a polynomial number of iterations, get an answer with poly(k) bits of precision. This algorithm is of independent interest. We apply it in the following manner. Several theoretical concerns about small algebraic coefficients can be reduced to testing functional inequalities. Our new bound then requires new results in the polynomial method for integer coefficients.

A: The polynomial approach is one of the earliest methods in mathematical number theory, introduced by Jacoby in the 19th century and introduced by Cassels in the 20th century.  It is often applied to problems about polynomial curves.  The fundamental concept is to decompose coefficients into products of coefficients of higher degree by looking at their roots.
In a nutshell: given an algebraic polynomial f(x) (where y is a finite extension of the integers, such as the integers modulo some prime) you try to factor it.  But it is even better to factor a degree $n$ polynomial if each of the roots of $p(x)$ is also of order $O(n^\epsilon)$ for some $\epsilon > 0$ rather than of order $O(\sqrt n)$.  If a "good" root of this order exists, it is usually difficult to identify it; but a "bad" root is more easy to get...

This paper introduces an innovative approach to one-shot image semantic segmentation through semantically meaningful class prototype learning. The study explores the limitations of existing one-shot segmentation methods, which typically lack consistency in handling semantic information due to limited examples. A new methodological framework is proposed, where class-specific prototypes are learned through an end-to-end trainable network to capture deeper, more robust semantic meanings underlining the classes. Comprehensive experimental evaluations on several challenging benchmark datasets demonstrate the superior performance and advantage of the proposed method. The study enriches the academic discourse on semantic segmentation and offers valuable insights for potential applications in visual content interpretation and information retrieval.

The continuous growth of signal processing on graphs offers a new
approach for processing large-scale data associated with irregular domains.
In certain practical systems, it is possible to handle large data sets
through complex systems, in which most nodes have limited computing power.
Designing optimal distributed algorithms is important for this task. This
paper relies on the distributed reconstruction of a time-varying bandlimited
graph signal based on measurements sampled at a subset of selected vertices. A
distributed least square reconstruction (DLSR) algorithm is proposed to retrieve
the unknown signal iteratively, by enabling neighboring nodes to communicate
with one another and make fast updates. DLSR provides a decay scheme to annihilate
the out-of-band energy occurring in the reconstruction process, which is
usually triggered by the transmission delay in distributed networks. Proof of
convergence and error bounds for DLSR are given in this paper, indicating
that the algorithms is unable to track time-varying graph signals and perfectly
reconstruct time-invariant signals. The DLSR algorithm is numerically
experimented with synthetic databases and real-world sensor network data, which
proves its ability in tracking rapidly time-varying graph signals.

During the last few years, a significant understanding has emerged of some of the basic physical and dynamic aspects that determine the behavior of low-energy chemical explosives under high pressures, so-called weak detonators (LDFD, 2D, 3M, etc.), during the transition from deflagration to detonation. Starting from the empirically obtained characteristic speed v*, several authors1–4 have argued that for various shock waves, these explosives will undergo DDT. Experimentally, it has been found that at high temperatures in the deflagration zone, at temperatures above 600°K, the speed of this DDT reaction (the high-T branch) differs significantly from the expected theoretical one, which indicates that something new is taking place under these conditions. To describe the high-T regime by the theoretical method, one must use more precise models rather than the simple models used in the previous studies of this problem, because at extremely high temperatures the role of lattice heat conductivity plays an important role. The goal of this work is to obtain an understanding of the two-step process by which the shock wave develops. We begin with a linear Eulerian description of a shock wave in a cylinder, enveloping the charge. As the shock wave moves forward, its intensity weakens, which leads to a deflation front, followed by a recompression shock wave, after which an exothermic reaction takes place, which produces a hot zone and finally leads to the full oxidation of the explosive medium, which produces detonation waves. We have derived in closed form all the speeds at different stages, up to the point when the solution reaches supersonic speeds. Using the results of the calculations in our new approach, it would be very instructive to study the role of the thermodynamically stable states in the whole evolution. For example, would they contribute to a lower pressure but become harmful at a higher one?

The families EPT (resp. EPG) Edge Intersection Graphs of Paths in a tree
(resp. in a grid) are well studied graph classes. Recently we introduced the
graph classes Edge-Intersecting and Non-Splitting Paths in a Tree ENPT, and in
a Grid (ENPG). It was shown that ENPG contains an infinite heirarchy of
subclasses that are obtained by restricting the number of bends in the paths.
Motivated by this result, in this work we focus on one bend {ENPG} graphs. We
show that one bend ENPG graphs are properly included in two bend ENPG graphs.
We also show that trees and cycles are one bend ENPG graphs, and characterize
the split graphs and co-bipartite graphs that are one bend ENPG. We prove that
the recognition problem of one bend ENPG split graphs is NP-complete even in a
very restricted subfamily of split graphs. Last we provide a linear time
recognition algorithm for one bend ENPG co-bipartite graphs.

The CNN-based techniques have demonstrated excellent results in medical image
segmentation, but it failed to capture the long-range dependencies due to the
inherent locality of convolution operation. Transformer-based techniques are
popular in vision tasks recently because of its capacity of long-range
dependencies and get a promising performance. Unfortunately, it lacks in modeling
local context, since some works tried to embed convolutional layer to
resolve this issue and achieved some progress, but it keeps the feature
inconsistent and fails to leverage the natural multi-scale features of
hierarchical transformer, which limit the performance of models. In this paper,
giving surgical image segmentation as an analogy, we present MISSFormer, an
inexpensive and powerful Clinical Picture Segmentation tranSFormer. MISSFormer is a
hierarchical encoder-decoder network and has three appealing designs: 1) A feed
forward network is designed with the proposed Advanced Transformer Block,
which keeps features aligned adaptively and supports the long-range
dependencies and local context. 2) We proposed Enhanced Transformer Context
Bridge, a context bridge with the enhanced transformer block to model the
long-range dependencies and local context of multi-scale features produced by
our hierarchical transformer encoder. Driven by these two designs, the
MISSFormer displays strong capability to capture more important dependencies and
context in medical image segmentation. The studies on multi-organ and
cardiac segmentation tasks show the superiority, reliability and
efficiency of our MISSFormer, the exprimental results of MISSFormer trained
from scratch often outperforms state-of-the-art techniques pretrained on ImageNet,
and the core designs can be generalized to other spatial segmentation tasks. The
code will be published in Github.

In this  study we propose an automatic  fish  species identification system that combines three different approaches to  achieve high accuracy in classification results; namely (1) Robust Feature Selection technique which selects  only relevant features with respect their  importance degree values calculated by   ReliefF algorithm [2], then these selected important  attributes are used as input data set into   two classifiers such ANN classifier[3]and DT Classifier.[4]. The proposed method   was  tested over four datasets collected at Al-Azhar University  Aquatic Research Center located near Cairo city  Egypt where each dataset contains images representing five  Egyptian freshwater fishes belonging either Cyprinidae or Siluriformes families. In addition,the performance  evaluation  metrics    were computed including Accuracy rate(AR), Precision  Rate PR ),  RecallRate RR )  Fmeasurement FM).  Results show very good performances  achieved when compared  against other stateofart  methods  reported previously especially   regarding AR value reaching  up 99% while maintaining low false alarm rates less than 1%. This indicates how effective our approach can be applied successfully  even  under challenging conditions like those encountered during  field surveys involving large numbers samples taken randomly across wide geographical areas without  any prior knowledge about what  kind specific organism might occur there beforehand."

Abstract:
In this paper, we continue the study of normal numbers, investigating the complexity of several normal number properties. We show that the Fourier transform of a normal number can be computed in polynomial time, and that the smallest representation of a normal number in a given number field can be found in polynomial time. We also show that these problems are NP-hard when the input is a set of integers that are not required to be normal. Additionally, we investigate the problem of determining the correctness of a given representation of a normal number, and show that this problem is also NP-hard. Finally, we examine the problem of finding a representation of a normal number in a given number field that satisfies a set of additional constraints, and show that this problem is NP-complete.

Abstract The fuzzy-possibilistic cluster methods (FPCMs) are very effective in clustering large sets of unknown or partially observed data, and their standardization allows them to be successfully applied on many applications. However, there has been relatively little attention paid the method's validity and reliability properties compared with many other clustering techniques. In this study we investigate what aspects make up these values and identify which facets of its methodology have the greatest intrinsic value when used for building robust statistical models that require unbiased predictions. We discuss some issues to consider when designing predictive models and present algorithm based on CMC-based fuzzy-possibiliistic clustering developed as part of our lab experiment that was validated using a large collection of real data sets. When correctly adapted it leads to extremely accurate discriminative accuracy which includes both classification of samples and non-classification of samples as to whether they belong to one set or another. Furthermore, while approach performs well in most cases to classify pairs of known samples into two distinct clusters, performance can nevertheless suffer when large number of data sample is being clustered along each component, especially if it involves low-dimensional data sets.

> In this work, we propose an inexpensive and inexpensive approach to segment gastric cells in endoscopic images with partial labels. We first extract patches around each labeled point as positive samples and then use them to train a algorithm which can be used to predict the label of unlabeled points. Unfortunately, due to the limited number of training data, it is impossible to provide correct predictions by employing only one round of learning. To address this issue, we introduce a reiterative learning approach that systematically updates the prediction results derived on the prior ones until convergence. Experimental findings show that our proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art methods on two public datasets.

This paper describes a method for segmenting images into component images which is intended to be used to detect nuclei in elongated images. We propose a method based on a combination of two image segmentation methods: thresholding and image partition by distance. We show that this method is robust in the presence of image artifacts, and that it can also be used to find nuclei in poorly sampled images. We also show that the method can be used to detect nuclei in poorly sampled images. We also show that the method can be used to detect nuclei in poorly sampled images. We also show that the method can be used to detect nuclei in poorly sampled images. We also show that the method can be used to detect nuclei in poorly sampled images. We also show that the method can be used to detect nuclei in poorly sampled images. We also show that the method can be used to detect nuclei in poorly sampled images. > Abstract: We present FPGA based data readout system for the pixel detector in the upgraded Belle II experiment at KEKB accelerator, Japan. This new system will be used to replace the existing CAMAC based system which has been running since 1998 and was designed with the original Belle detector. In this paper we describe design considerations that were taken into account when designing new system as well as its architecture and implementation details.

In this paper, we investigate the role  of viscous effects  during reconnection in solar jets using data from  the Hinode/Solar   Optical Telescope.  We find that the jets exhibit oscillatory behavior, with a period  of approximately  5 minutes. We interpret this as due to  the presence of a viscous timescale in the  reconnection  process. This result has implications for our understanding of  reconnection in astrophysical plasmas.

Colorectal cancer (CRC) is first cause of death in many countries. CRC
originates from small clump of cells on lining of the colon called
polyps, which over time might grow and become malignant. Early detection and
removal of polyps are therefore necessary for the prevention of colon cancer.
In this paper, we introduce an ensemble of medical polyp segmentation
algorithms. Based on an observation that different segmentation algorithms will
perform well on different subsets of examples because of the nature and size of
training sets they have been exposed to and because of method-intrinsic
factors, we propose to measure confidence in the prediction of each
algorithm and then use an associate threshold to determine whether the
confidence is acceptable or not. An algorithm is selected for ensemble if
the confidence is below its associate threshold. The optimal threshold for each
segmentation algorithm is found by using Comprehensive Learning Particle Swarm
Optimization (CLPSO), swarm intelligence algorithm. The Dice coefficient, a
popular performance metric for image segmentation, is used as fitness
criteria. Experimental results on two polyp segmentation datasets MICCAI2015
and Kvasir-SEG confirm that our ensemble achieves better results compared to
some well-known segmentation algorithms.

In this paper, we explore the connection between high order three-term recursions, Riemann-Hilbert minors, and Nikishin systems on star-like sets. We present a novel approach to studying the solvability of these systems by leveraging the properties of Riemann-Hilbert minors and their relationship to the underlying combinatorial structure of the star-like sets. Our main result establishes a necessary and sufficient condition for the solvability of a high order three-term recursion on a star-like set in terms of the existence of a particular type of Riemann-Hilbert minor. 

We demonstrate the power of this result by applying it to a variety of examples, including the study of Nikishin systems, which are a class of integrable systems that arise in various areas of mathematics and physics. Our work provides a significant advancement in the field of algebraic combinatorics and has important implications for the study of integrable systems. 

The novel techniques and results presented in this paper open up new avenues for research in this area and demonstrate the rich interplay between combinatorics, algebra, and analysis. 

Keywords: high order three-term recursions, Riemann-Hilbert minors, Nikishin systems, star-like sets, algebraic combinatorics, integrable systems.

We define a weakly threshold sequence to be a degree sequence
$d=(d_1,\dots,d_n)$ of a graph having the property that $\sum_{i \leq k} d_i
\geq e(k-1)+\sum_{i > j} \min\{j,d_i\} - 1$ for all positive $k \leq
\max\{i:d_i \geq i-1\}$. The weakly threshold graphs are the realizations of
the weakly limit sequences. The weakly threshold graphs properly include
the threshold graph and satisfy pleasing extensions of certain properties of
threshold graph. We demonstrate a majorization property of weakly limit
sequences and an iterative construction algorithm for weakly threshold graphs,
as well as a forbidden induced subgraph characterization. We conclude by
just describing weakly limit sequences and graphs.

In this paper we introduce the notion of nonlocality for dimer algebras, and show that it is a natural extension of the corresponding concept for quantum groups. We show that the nonlocality of a dimer algebra is determined by its central geometry, and use this to give a classification of all nonlocal dimer algebras. This classification is used to show that thenonlocality of a dimer algebra can be detected by its Hochschild cohomology.

In recent years,  data augmentation has  become a popular technique in deep learning to improve model performance on unseen domains. However, this approach assumes that models need to generalize across all  possible  unseen domains, which may not always be necessary  or desirable.  This paper explores  when  and why it  might be unnecessary to use domain-generalizing techniques such as data augmentation, by analyzing the trade-off between domain adaptation and overfitting. We propose a  framework for evaluating  the effectiveness of different data augmentation strategies based on their ability to balance  these competing  objectives.  Our   results  show that some types of data  augmentation  can actually lead to worse performance  on unseen domains than  using no augmentation at all, highlighting the importance of carefully  selecting appropriate methods for each specific task.  Overall, our  work  provides valuable insights into the limitations of  current approaches  to  domain adaptation and suggests new directions for future  research.

This research presents U-Net, a deep learning model based on convolutional neural systems (CNNs) built primarily for biomedical image segmentation. The design of U-Net provides a contracting path to capture context and an expansive path that requires precise translation. Experimental results show excellent efficiency compared with other state-of-the art techniques in analyzing molecular systems such as neurons and cells from microscopy images. Overall, this work highlights the potential of CNNs coupled with U-Net's innovative architecture for precise and efficient automated analysis of large datasets in various fields of science and science.

TFLMS is a system that provides Large Model Support in TensorFlow by using graph rewriting techniques. It allows for efficient training and inference of large neural networks, which are becoming increasingly popular in various applications such as natural language processing and computer vision. TFLMS addresses the challenges of memory and computational limitations by breaking down the large model into smaller sub-graphs that can be executed in parallel. The system has been evaluated on various large models and has shown significant improvements in both training and inference time. Overall, TFLMS is a valuable tool for researchers and practitioners working with large neural networks in TensorFlow.

This paper presents a semi-supervised method for left atrium (LA) segmentation with mutual consistency training (MCT). In medical imageing analysis, accurate and efficient segmentation of the LA from 3D cardiovascular Magnetic Resonance (MR) images is essential, but often hindered by limited labelled data and large intra-class variations. Our approach capitalises on the strength of available labelled and unlabelled data to train a deep neural network. We introduce a novel approach, termed mutual consistency training (MCT), which encourages the predictions for the same unlabelled data under different views to be consistent. Extensive experiments on the public LA challenge dataset demonstrate the superiority of our method over existing state-of-the-art semi-supervised methods. Our approach shows considerable improvement in segmentation accuracy and can be used as a fundamental tool in automatic LA segmentation in MR images, contributing substantially to cardiovascular disease research and diagnosis.

Let s_q(n) be the base-q sum of digits, which for n=x is centred on (q-1)/2 log x. In Dromota, Mauduit and Rivat's paper they give the asymptotics for the width of the interval p  x, p prime: s_q(p) = alpha (q-1)  x for p  p  p-1, where p is a prime. To get this lower bound they make use of the sharp peak of the multinomial distribution and of the fact that there are infinitely many primes whose expansion in base-2 has more 1s than 0s.

Wе dеmоnstrаtе thаt bу аррlуіng twо-рhоtоn ехсіtаtіоn sсhеmеs іt bесоmеs роssіblе nоt оnlу sіgnіfісаntlу іnсrеаsе sіgnаl strеngth but аlsо suррrеss unwаntеd еffесts suсh us Dоррlеr brоаdеnіng оr Ζееmаn shіfts саusеd е.g.,bу mаgnеtіс fіеlds іnduсеd durіng орtісаl рumріng рrосеssеs usеd рrіоr аtоm dеtесtіоn vіа аbsоrрtіоn sресtrоsсору tесhnіquеs lіkе SΝSΡD bаsеd sіnglе рhоtоn соuntіng [1]. Recent advances in semantic image segmentation have mostly been achieved by
training deep convolutional neural networks (CNNs). We show how to improve
semantic segmentation through the use of contextual information; specifically,
we explore `patch-patch' context between image regions, and `patch-background'
context. For learning from the patch-patch context, we formulate Conditional
Random Fields (CRFs) with CNN-based pairwise potential functions to capture
semantic correlations between neighboring patches. Efficient piecewise training
of the proposed deep structured model is then applied to avoid repeated
expensive CRF inference for back propagation. For capturing the
patch-background context, we show that a network design with traditional
multi-scale image input and sliding pyramid pooling is effective for improving
performance. Our experimental results set new state-of-the-art performance on a
number of popular semantic segmentation datasets, including NYUDv2, PASCAL VOC
8893, PASCAL-Context, and SIFT-flow. In particular, we achieve an
intersection-over-union score of 78.0 on the challenging PASCAL VOC 7346
dataset.

The Encoder-Decoder architecture is a main stream deep learning model for
biomedical image segmentation. The encoder fully compresses the input and
generates encoded features, and the decoder then produces dense predictions
using encoded features. However, decoders are still under-explored in such
architectures. In this paper, we comprehensively study the state-of-the-art
Encoder-Decoder architectures, and propose a new universal decoder, called
cascade decoder, to improve semantic segmentation accuracy. Our cascade decoder
can be embedded into existing networks and trained altogether in an end-to-end
fashion. The cascade decoder structure aims to conduct more effective decoding
of hierarchically encoded features and is more compatible with common encoders
than the known decoders. We replace the decoders of state-of-the-art models
with our cascade decoder for several challenging biomedical image segmentation
tasks, and the considerable improvements achieved demonstrate the efficacy of
our new decoding method.

"In this work, we demonstrate how a quantum tensor network model can be used to address the problem of image segmentation. Specifically, we construct a variational quantum circuit model for segmenting medical images, and demonstrate that it outperforms state-of-the-art methods. Our proposal has the potential to be a valuable component in the development of medical imaging technologies.
A variational quantum circuit model for segmenting medical images
We demonstrate that a variational quantum circuit model can be used to segment medical images. In particular, we construct a model consisting of unitary entanglings gates and single-qubit rotations that is used to determine the optimal parameters in order to obtain an accurate segmentation.
We show that our proposed model is robust to noise and is accurate across a range of parameter values. We also demonstrate that the model has good performance in terms of accuracy and efficiency, making it suitable for use in practical settings.
To test the performance of our model, we compare it to several other methods. We show that, when given access to the ground truth, our model outperforms all other methods in terms of both accuracy and computational cost. This makes our model well-suited for applications where the ground truth is not readily available.
Overall, we propose a quantum algorithm that significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods for segmenting medical images. Our method allows for accurate and efficient segmentation, and is flexible enough to be applied to a variety of imaging scenarios."
A research paper titled 'Physics-inspired variational quantum circuits for image segmentation' and published in Physical Review Research, introduces the idea of the physical-quantum connection and shows that image segmentation can be performed on variational quantum circuits. The authors developed a model that can be used to approximate an image based on its geometry and other geometric features, and applied it to medical applications. By training the model in a supervised fashion, they were able to achieve high accuracy in segmentation. Additionally, they provided a theoretical framework for studying the behavior of the model in different scenarios.
One of the authors, Sumeet Kumar, said, "My motivation for this research comes from the field of artificial intelligence. As our world becomes increasingly digital, the need for accurate image recognition and segmentation approaches is becoming more and more pressing. With the advent of quantum computing, we now have the potential to dramatically accelerate our progress in these areas."

Lеt $\mаthbf{G}$ bе а rеduсtіvе grоuр dеfіnеd оvеr $\mаthbb{Q}$ аnd lеt
$\mаthfrаk{S}$ bе а Sіеgеl sеt іn $\mаthbf{G}(\mаthbb{R})$. Тhе Sіеgеl рrореrtу
tеlls us thаt thеrе аrе оnlу fіnіtеlу mаnу $\gаmmа \іn \mаthbf{G}(\mаthbb{Q})$
оf bоundеd dеtеrmіnаnt аnd dеnоmіnаtоr fоr whісh thе trаnslаtе
$\gаmmа.\mаthfrаk{S}$ іntеrsесts $\mаthfrаk{S}$. Wе рrоvе а bоund fоr thе
hеіght оf thеsе $\gаmmа$ whісh іs роlуnоmіаl wіth rеsресt tо thе dеtеrmіnаnt
аnd dеnоmіnаtоr. Τhе bоund gеnеrаlіsеs а rеsult оf Наbеggеr аnd Ріlа dеаlіng
wіth thе саsе оf $\mаthbf{GL}_2$, аnd hаs аррlісаtіоns tо thе Ζіlbеr--Ріnk
соnjесturе оn unlіkеlу іntеrsесtіоns іn Shіmurа vаrіеtіеs.
  Ιn аddіtіоn wе рrоvе thаt іf $\mаthbf{Н}$ іs а subgrоuр оf $\mаthbf{G}$, thеn
еvеrу Sіеgеl sеt fоr $\mаthbf{Η}$ іs соntаіnеd іn а fіnіtе unіоn оf
$\mаthbf{G}(\mаthbb{Q})$-trаnslаtеs оf а Sіеgеl sеt fоr $\mаthbf{G}$).

Тhіs рареr рrеsеnts а nоvеl unssuреrvіsеd іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn mеthоd bаsеd оn glоbаl аnd lосаl сrіtеrіа орtіmіzаtіоn vіа Βауеsіаn Νеtwоrks. Тhе рrороsеd mеthоd аіms tо оvеrсоmе thе dіffісultіеs еnсоuntеrеd wіth trаdіtіоnаl mеthоds whісh fаіl tо аdеquаtеlу bаlаnсе glоbаl аnd lосаl іnfоrmаtіоn, lеаdіng tо lеss ассurаtе sеgmеntаtіоn. Οur mеthоd еmрlоуs Βауеsіаn Νеtwоrks tо mоdеl thе іntеrdереndеnсіеs bеtwееn ріхеls, thеrеfоrе аllоwіng thе sіmultаnеоus орtіmіzаtіоn оf glоbаl аnd lосаl сrіtеrіа. Τо еvаluаtе іts реrfоrmаnсе, wе аррlіеd thе рrороsеd mеthоd tо sеvеrаl sуnthеtіс аnd rеаl-wоrld іmаgеs, аnd thе rеsults wеrе соmраrеd wіth оthеr еstаblіshеd іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn mеthоds. Тhе ехреrіmеntаl rеsults dеmоnstrаtе thаt оur рrороsеd mеthоd рrоvіdеs sіgnіfісаntlу іmрrоvеd sеgmеntаtіоn quаlіtу іn tеrms оf рrесіsіоn, rесаll, аnd ассurасу mеаsurеmеnts.

In recent years, deep learning models have been proposed for improving  the performance of multi-modal learning tasks, such as image  and video classification. These models  usually learn  to combine the features from different  modalities in a data-driven  fashion. In this  paper, we propose a new method for improving the performance of  multi-modal learning tasks by   using a uni-modal teacher model. The  proposed method is based  on the idea of transfer learning,  where the knowledge  learned by the uni-modal teacher model is transferred to the multi-modal student model.  The uni-modal teacher  model is trained  on  a  large dataset  that is annotated with labels from a single  modality. The  labels from the other modalities are synthesized using the  uni-modal  teacher model. The synthesized labels are used  to  train the multi-modal student model. The proposed method is evaluated on   three publicly  available datasets: MNIST, CIFAR-10, and ImageNet. The results show that the proposed method outperforms the state-of-the-art methods on  all three datasets.

Abstract:
We study the  question  of whether a finite number of elements $x_1,  \ldots, x_n$ in a  finite-dimensional algebra $A$ always  have a common eigenvector, by constructing an $A  \otimes  A \otimes \cdots \otimes  A^{\otimes n}$ module containing all  the common  eigenspaces,  and analysing  its graded structure. In particular, this provides necessary and sufficient conditions for  commuting elements to have common eigenvectors in an arbitrary block of nilpotents of a   quiver algebra.

The article  was authored by Igor  A. Rodnianski (https://math.berkeley.edu/~rodnianski/) and published by  the American  Mathematical Society. The PDF version of the paper is available at http://www.math.berkeley.edu/~rodnianski/files/AlgebraicDependence.pdf. The version at https://arxiv.org/abs/1610.04512 is currently  unavailable.
See also http://matd1.math.uni-bielefeld.de/d1d/pers/rodnianski.html.

For instance, in the case A  is commutative, the algebra  S xS is generated in degree 1 if and only if  S is algebraically independent.
In this  paper  we consider the following question: (i) When  (a)  does the subalgebra of the commutant generated by S commute with itself?
(b)  do all elements of S have common eigenvectors?
It appears to be an interesting and important question with important  applications, both in mathematics and physics. However, in  many  cases it is much more difficult then  the commutative case.

Recent years have Seen tremendous progress in still-image segmentation;
however the na\"ive application of these state-of-the-art algorithms to every
video frame requires considerable computation and ignores the temporal
continuity inherent in video. We propose a video recognition framework that
relies on two Key observations: 1) while pixels may change rapidly from frame
to frame, the semantic content of a scene evolves more slowly, and 2) execution
Can be viewed as an aspect of architecture, yielding purpose-fit computation
schedules for Networks. We define a novel family of "clockwork" convnets driven
by fixed or adaptive clock signals that schedule the processing Of different
layers at different update rates according to their semantic stability. We
design a pipeline Schedule to reduce latency for real-time recognition and a
fixed-rate schedule to reduce overall computation. Finally, we extend clockwork
scheduling to adaptive video processing by incorporating data-driven clocks
That can be tuned on unlabeled video. The accuracy and efficiency of clockwork
convnets are evaluated on the Youtube-Objects, nYUD, and Cityscapes video
datasets.

Abstract:
In this paper, we establish a relationship between the Galois group and the dynamical properties of unicritical polynomials. By considering the action of the Galois group on the set of complex numbers, we show that the fixed points of the Galois group correspond to the periodic points of the polynomial. This correspondence allows us to provide a new proof of the fundamental theorem of algebra, as well as to develop a theory of dynamical Galois groups. Furthermore, we explore the connections between the Galois group and the set of complex numbers, and we demonstrate how the Galois group can be used to solve polynomial equations. Our results provide a new perspective on the relationship between algebra and dynamics, and they have applications in both fields.

In many real world applications, the outcome of a competition is often determined using single elimination tournaments. For  example, national sports teams usually play competitions to determine  which team will    represent its  country in international sporting events  such as  the  World Cup or  the Olympics. Also, college fairs frequently  use single elimination tournaments  to allow representatives from colleges and universities to  demonstrate to and compete against each  other for  potential  students.

The main question that  we aim  to answer in  this paper  is this: given k competitors with corresponding abilities, a and b, respectively, where 0 ≤  a, b ≤1  are  the best  and worst ability respectively,   can one of the competitors win against all other competitors such that the winner (W) has an ability that  is less than  the competitor he  has  to   play against in the final (F)? We show that the answer to this question is no. In other words, it is not  always possible, given the competition of these k competitors, for their abilities to be such that there exists a winning way for a competitor to have the lowest ability.

Our result also  generalizes  to k competitors with corresponding abilities, a and b, respectively,  where a < b. We demonstrate that, unless there is   a significant gap in strengths between the most  likely competitor to  win (M) and the competitor with  the highest ability (H), the same pattern of  tournament results applies. The most  likely competitor to win, M, is  often considered as the competitor that  is best able to compete  with any other competitor.

Furthermore, the assumption  about k competitors is  also generalized to  more     than k competitors. We  show that it is also possible for  W to have an  ability that is greater than or equal to the  ability   of F as  long  as  the tournament has more than k participants,  the competitors  are placed randomly,  and there is no specific  competitor whose ability is better than all others.

The most important  implication of our result is   that, in  single elimination tournaments, the best  is not always the winner. Therefore, in such tournaments, the choice of the winner should be based not only  on  the competitor's ability  but also on other variables such as the ability distribution,  the competitor's rankings, and their relative ranking.

This paper presents a novel deep learning framework called PC-U Net for jointly reconstructing and segmenting the cardiac walls in 3D from CT data. The proposed framework utilizes a U-Net architecture with a novel point cloud representation to effectively capture the spatial information of the cardiac walls. The network is trained using a combination of reconstruction and segmentation loss functions to ensure accurate and consistent results. Experimental results on a large dataset demonstrate that the PC-U Net outperforms existing methods in terms of both reconstruction and segmentation accuracy. The proposed framework has the potential to improve the diagnosis and treatment planning of cardiac diseases.

> With the emergence of lightweight and efficient 3D convolutional neural networks (CNNs), they have broad applications in diverse real-world scenarios. 

However, how to obtain an ultra-efficient 3D CNN based on efficient building blocks with better accuracy remains a challenge. 

In this paper, we propose a novel Channel-wise Automatic KErnel Shrinking (CAKES) method for efficient 3D CNN design, in which a high-level search framework is applied to develop the CAKES-based 3D CNN. 

Specifically, the CAKES method is implemented by automatically and efficiently selecting the optimal 3D convolution kernels to achieve the superior model accuracy with much fewer parameters, while minimizing the computational complexity of the corresponding 3D kernels. Extensive experiments on three well-known 3D datasets demonstrate that the CAKES-based CNN models can achieve higher accuracy with a remarkable speedup when compared with the state-of-the-art 3D CNNs. 

Introduction
In recent years, the applications of 3D convolutional neural networks (3D CNNs) in various tasks of medical image processing, autonomous driving, etc., have emerged. To achieve high accuracy in different real-world scenarios [1][2][3][4], efficient 3D CNN architectures with reasonable resource consumption are required (with less memory consumption and computational complexity). 

For instance, some of the common 3D CNNs are MobileNet V2 [5] and ShuffleNet V2 [6], which require fewer parameters and less computation because they apply group convolution to reduce the number of output channels in each layer and then shrink the feature maps from the original input channel size to a fixed size. However, because the above lightweight 3D CNNs are manually designed and optimized, their performance is sub-optimal. To obtain an ultra-efficient model with a higher accuracy, how to design an automated efficient 3D CNN architecture is still a challenge. To address this challenge, neural architecture search (NAS) [7] attracts extensive research attention because of its high-efficiency and remarkable performance. The aim of NAS is to automatically find a neural network that can outperform the manually designed ones. Currently, NAS has been demonstrated to be powerful and attractive in the field of convolutional neural networks (CNN)-based image recognition.

The high beam current and sub-angstrom resolution of aberration-corrected
scanning transmission electron microscopes has enabled electron energy loss
spectroscopic (EELS) mapping with atomic resolution. These spectral maps are
often dose-limited and spatially oversampled, leading to low counts/channel and
are thus highly sensitive to errors in background estimation. However, by
taking advantage of redundancy in the dataset map one can improve background
estimation and increase chemical sensitivity. We consider two such approaches-
linear combination of power laws and local background averaging-that reduce
background error and improve signal extraction. Principal components analysis
(PCA) can also be used to analyze spectrum images, but the poor
peak-to-background ratio in EELS can lead to serious artifacts if raw EELS data
is PCA filtered. We identify common artifacts and discuss alternative
approaches. These algorithms are implemented within the Cornell Spectrum
Imager, an open source software package for spectroscopic analysis.

In this paper we present experimental data underlying a new osmotic law and its derivation. The van’t Hoff equation, which relates the osmotic pressure of a solution to its concentration and temperature, has been used in many fields for over a century. Recent studies have shown, however, that the equation has limitations and is not free from inaccuracies, especially at high concentrations and temperatures. Our new law, which is based on a novel method that takes solvent–solvent interactions into account, is a more accurate and general formula for osmotic pressure, which is also simple and easy to apply. We have tested the formula with several experimental methods including membrane osmometry, freezing point depression and vapor pressure osmometry. Our results demonstrate that our formula is a better fit to the experimental data than the van’t Hoff equation, especially at high temperatures and concentrations. We have compared our formula with other recent proposals for an improved van’t Hoff equation and have found it to be the most accurate and simple to apply. The significance of our work is that it can now be used as a more reliable tool for predicting and controlling osmotic pressure in a wide variety of practical applications, including water treatment and desalination. Our new formula can also be used to improve the accuracy of calculations in chemical engineering, materials science and pharmaceutical applications, where osmotic pressure is critical. We are convinced that our new formula will have a major influence on the field of osmometry and its related disciplines and that it will become a standard tool for academics and professionals in the future.

important variable which are relevant to the response variable of interest.
Variable selection and the multiple testing are both tools to address this
issue. However, there is little discussion on the connection of these two
areas. When the signal strength is strong enough such that the selection
consistency is achievable, it seems to be unnecessary to control the false
discovery rate. In this paper, we consider the regime where the signals are
both rare and weak such that the selection consistency is not achievable and
propose a method which controls the false discovery rate asymptotically. It is
theoretically shown that the false non-discovery rate of the proposed method
converges to zero at the optimal rate. Numerical results are provided to
demonstrate the advantage of the proposed method.

Resource allocation, namely, power and training length allocation, in MIMO radar with multiple targets for non-coherent localization is discussed. Through numerical results, we demonstrate the inefficiency of a constant resource allocation strategy, which assigns the same power and training length to all antennas, and show the importance of adaptive resource allocation. We then propose a resource allocation strategy that consists of three steps: 1) discovery of angular regions that are conducive for target localization; 2) prioritization of these angular regions in resource allocation; 2) allocation of resources in order of priority. The proposed strategy is shown to achieve higher detection probability and more accurate localization than the constant strategy for various scenarios through numerical results.

> "We introduce an unsupervised learning method that can achieve semantic segmentations on 90+ ImageNet classes given only a small target domain training set -- no class labels, object bounds or auxiliary data required. First we build a teacher-student pipeline using adversarial learning to ensure strong perceptual fidelity of synthesized images (a challenging dataset constraint). Next, to improve synthesis accuracy by incorporating external knowledge about fine details like natural colors & shapes across various contexts, instead of supervision we incorporate contrastive embeddings between source domains images as additional discriminative information loss terms during GAN training. Since our student network captures global and regional style factors while preserving the shape content from both real and synthetic sample sets together, this framework provides superior semantic masks over prior methods."

Ιn thе wоrld оf mоdеrn fіnаnсіаl thеоrу, роrtfоlіо соnstruсtіоn hаs
trаdіtіоnаllу ореrаtеd undеr аt lеаst оnе оf twо сеntrаl аssumрtіоns: thе
соnstrаіnts аrе dеrіvеd frоm а utіlіtу funсtіоn аnd/оr thе multіvаrіаtе
рrоbаbіlіtу dіstrіbutіоn оf thе undеrlуіng аssеt rеturns іs fullу knоwn. Ιn
рrасtісе, bоth thе реrfоrmаnсе сrіtеrіа аnd thе іnfоrmаtіоnаl struсturе аrе
mаrkеdlу dіffеrеnt: rіsk-tаkіng аgеnts аrе mаndаtеd tо buіld роrtfоlіоs bу
рrіmаrіlу соnstrаіnіng thе tаіls оf thе роrtfоlіо rеturn tо sаtіsfу VаR, strеss
tеstіng, оr ехресtеd shоrtfаll (СVаR) соndіtіоns, аnd аrе lаrgеlу іgnоrаnt
аbоut thе rеmаіnіng рrореrtіеs оf thе рrоbаbіlіtу dіstrіbutіоns. Аs аn
аltеrnаtіvе, wе dеrіvе thе shаре оf роrtfоlіо dіstrіbutіоns whісh hаvе mахіmum
еntrору subjесt tо rеаl-wоrld lеft-tаіl соnstrаіnts аnd оthеr ехресtаtіоns. Τwо
соnsеquеnсеs аrе (і) thе lеft-tаіl соnstrаіnts аrе suffісіеntlу роwеrful tо
